_meta.purpose: Deterministic, KEYLESS, LIST-BASED classification for the two THEMATIC instruments (EU Open Source Strategy -> 'Open source', Strategic Roadmap for Digitalisation & AI in Energy -> 'AI in energy'). These are THEMES, not equity sectors, so they are NOT keyword-guessed off the screener. A company is classified iff it appears on one of the rosters below. Used by scripts/classify_theme.py.
_meta.why_lists: Membership is the credibility line: 'included because it is an Eclipse Foundation member' / 'a smartEn flexibility member' is a citable, falsifiable claim — not a heuristic guess. Thematic companies may be added even if absent from STOXX 600.

rules.match: A roster member is classified into the roster's category. rationale = 'Member of <roster.list>'. confidence = 1.0 (membership is binary).
rules.ticker_cross_reference: For each member, classify_theme.py resolves a Yahoo ticker via yfinance where the entity is a listed equity (so Layer-1 live financials work). If private / a foundation / a public body, it is added as a static, clearly-labeled non-listed entry (is_listed:false, financials:null) — never invented price data.
rules.provenance: source = 'auto-theme'; rationale = roster name; confidence = 1.0. sovereignty_role on the created record is set to the auto rationale and labeled auto-derived.
rules.reconciliation: On each run, classify_theme.py refreshes each roster's members[] from its source_url — a PLAIN HTTP fetch + parse of the public member directory, NOT a model call — and updates last_checked. A member that drops off the live roster is demoted/removed on the next run (logged in universe_log.json).
rules.seed_status: members[] below are intentionally EMPTY in this first commit. The rosters, scopes, and source URLs are what you sanity-check now; classify_theme.py populates verified members from source_url on the first run after your OK. See member_schema for the entry shape.

member_schema.name: Company or organisation legal name
member_schema.hq_country: EU/EEA country (members outside EU/EEA are dropped)
member_schema.yahoo_ticker: Yahoo symbol incl. suffix, or null for private/foundation/public-body
member_schema.roster_source_url: the parent roster's source_url (carried onto the record's sources[])
member_schema.verified: true once confirmed against source_url on a run; false in hand-seeds
member_schema.note: optional, e.g. membership tier or relevance

rosters.scope: EU/EEA demand-side flexibility, DER and smart-grid software members
EU/EEA companies named as direct/indirect participants in approved IPCEIs touching energy digitalisation

rosters.list: EU Digitalisation & AI in Energy roadmap — named organisations
rosters.scope: organisations explicitly named in the roadmap / its annexes

AI in energy.notes: ENTSO-E membership is national TSOs (grid operators, mostly state-owned, not listed software vendors) — deliberately NOT used as a roster here; smartEn is the right software/flexibility roster. Can be added later if you want TSOs in scope.
